records.ety: [L. debilitatus, p. p. of debilitare to debilitate, fr. debilis. See Debility.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Debilitated; p. pr. & vb. n. Debilitating.]

defs: To impair the strength of; to weaken; to enfeeble; as, to debilitate the body by intemperance.
quotes: The debilitated frame of Mr. Bertram was exhausted by this last effort.
